posts_per_day_rationale: User-confirmed diminishing returns beyond 5 videos per day per channel; 5 slots is the hard ceiling, not a lower bound. Skill must never schedule a 6th slot on the same calendar day (it would starve the next day's queue and underperform engagement-wise).

workflow_setup.clip_length_preset_note: User requirement — only generate shorts with duration between 30s and 90s. On the /workflow page after upload, the Clip Length selector MUST be set to this range (not the default 'Auto (<90s)'). The 30-90s window covers the sweet spot for YT Shorts, Reels, and TikTok without tripping platform minimum-length enforcement.

drive_upload.note: Drive upload via drive_upload.py is the preferred ingest path — 3-click user handoff via Opus's in-page Drive picker is faster than navigating macOS file chooser. See opus-clips/drive_upload.py for usage.

notes: Times are local to `timezone`. Opus's scheduler UI expects times in the user's Opus account timezone. `viral_score_threshold` auto-selects clips ≥ this score. `pause_duration_threshold_s` controls the Remove-pauses slider (Phase C found the subpanel defaults to 0s, which over-trims; 0.5s preserves natural speech rhythm). Do NOT exceed `posts_per_day_cap` per day — user has confirmed diminishing returns above 5/day.
